title: hieracium saxifragum subsp. lepidolytes
intro: Hieracium saxifragum subsp. lepidolytes, also known as hawkweed, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion-like flowering plants in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Hieracium saxifragum subsp. lepidolytes include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and fever.
